headline: UK's BT and Nscale team up to build 14MW of AI capacity
summary: Britain's BT Group is teaming up with Nscale to ‌build up to 14 megawatts of ‌AI data centre capacity across three of its sites ​using Nvidia infrastructure, expanding its sovereign data service to public sector and business customers.  Nscale, founded in 2024 and backed by Nvidia, ‌owns and operates ⁠its own data centres and raised $2 billion last month in a ⁠funding round that valued it at $14.6 billion.  Sovereign computing refers to a country's capacity to develop and control its own AI infrastructure.

headline: SK Hynix quarterly profit jumps five-fold on strong AI chip demand
summary:
STORY: :: SK Hynix

South Korea's SK Hynix on Thursday posted a record quarterly profit, and forecast AI chip demand would exceed manufacturing capacity.

The Nvidia supplier reported an operating profit of just over $25 billion for January-March.

That's a five-fold jump from last year, and in line with analyst estimates.

Shares in the firm rose over 2% in morning trade, but later fell back into negative territory. 

The stock has jumped nearly 90% so far this year.

SK Hynix said that client requests for chips over the next three years already exceed production capacity.

The outlook signals strong momentum for the high bandwidth memory chips used in AI, likely keeping supplies constrained and prices high.

SK Hynix said earlier it was accelerating capacity expansion.

That includes bringing forward the opening of a chip plant in South Korea to meet surging demand.

It sees limited impact from the war in the Middle East, having taken steps to secure inventory, diversify suppliers and lock in long-term energy contracts.

content.title: SK Hynix quarterly profit jumps five-fold on strong AI chip demand
content.summary:
STORY: :: SK Hynix

South Korea's SK Hynix on Thursday posted a record quarterly profit, and forecast AI chip demand would exceed manufacturing capacity.

The Nvidia supplier reported an operating profit of just over $25 billion for January-March.

That's a five-fold jump from last year, and in line with analyst estimates.

Shares in the firm rose over 2% in morning trade, but later fell back into negative territory. 

The stock has jumped nearly 90% so far this year.

SK Hynix said that client requests for chips over the next three years already exceed production capacity.

The outlook signals strong momentum for the high bandwidth memory chips used in AI, likely keeping supplies constrained and prices high.

SK Hynix said earlier it was accelerating capacity expansion.

That includes bringing forward the opening of a chip plant in South Korea to meet surging demand.

It sees limited impact from the war in the Middle East, having taken steps to secure inventory, diversify suppliers and lock in long-term energy contracts.

headline: SK Hynix Posts 400% Profit Rise. Micron Stock Is Falling.
summary: SK Hynix  reported surging quarterly profit on the back of a memory-chip shortage that could last for years.  SK Hynix gave up initial gains to close up 0.2% in local trading while Micron was down 1.4%.  SK Hynix executives told analysts that demand for high-bandwidth memory—a crucial component for artificial-intelligence chips from the likes of  Nvidia  —outstrips current supply capacity for the next three years.
